title: Repo-Boundary Enforcement — Defense-in-Depth
goal: Layered enforcement so agents cannot drift outside the workspace: a static invariant (structural-check asserts external_directory == deny, preventing config regression) plus a runtime tool.execute.before plugin (repo-boundary.ts) that denies path-bearing tools and bash commands whose resolved path escapes {directory,worktree}, closing the scout-found bypass vectors (git -C, ls, subprocess, symlinks, last-match-wins allow).

success_criteria.check: Static invariant exists and fails on regression (structural-check Check 7 asserts external_directory == deny; exits 1 on ask/allow/missing)
success_criteria.verify: set external_directory=ask, bash .opencode/tool/structural-check.sh (exit 1); restore deny, re-run (exit 0)

success_criteria.check: Plugin exists, bounded (<=300 lines), SDK-only import
Plugin denies escapes and allows in-repo (>=6 bun test cases: in-repo pass, ../ escape throw, symlink-outside throw, git -C ../ throw, ls ../ throw, in-repo bash pass)
Plugin does not false-deny legitimate work (verify.sh runs with plugin live, exits 0)
Config cannot silently regress (static check + verify together catch a committed ask)
Decision recorded in MEMORY.md + bead closed (.active removed)

tasks.title: Static invariant — structural-check Check 7 (external_directory == deny)
tasks.end_state: structural-check.sh has a Check 7 asserting .opencode/opencode.json root permission.external_directory == deny via bun/jq; exits 1 on ask/allow/missing; check counter 6->7; verify.sh green.
tasks.verify: bash .opencode/tool/structural-check.sh (exit 0); set external_directory=ask, re-run (exit 1); restore deny; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh (exit 0)

tasks.title: Runtime repo-boundary plugin (tool.execute.before containment guard)
tasks.end_state: .opencode/plugin/repo-boundary.ts (<=300 lines, SDK-only) mirrors guard.ts: tool.execute.before reads {directory,worktree} from ctx, resolves path args (read/edit/glob/grep) + bash command tokens against directory with realpath, throws on escape, returns {args} unchanged otherwise; typecheck clean; verify.sh green (no false-deny).

tasks.title: Plugin behavioral tests (bun, >=6 cases with real temp dir + symlink)
tasks.end_state: .opencode/plugin/repo-boundary.test.ts passes: in-repo read/edit/glob/grep pass; ../sibling escape throws; symlink-to-outside throws; git -C ../sibling throws; ls ../sibling throws; cat in-repo-file passes. Uses a real temp dir + real symlink (no mocking of realpath).

tasks.title: Docs + closeout (AGENTS.md, README.md, MEMORY.md, roadmap.md, state.md, .active removed)
tasks.end_state: AGENTS.md + .opencode/README.md plugin inventories updated; MEMORY.md decision appended (Option C, what it catches, residual accepted bypass); roadmap.md experiment->shipped; state.md active+complete; verify.sh + plugin tests green; committed+pushed; .active git rm'd.

open_questions.question: Bash-scan granularity: token-scan vs targeted denylist (git -C/ls/subprocess)
open_questions.resolution: Resolve in Task 2: token-scan (simplest, catches all vectors); denylist is brittle

open_questions.question: Symlink policy: resolve+deny escape (strict) vs leave to opencode lexical
open_questions.resolution: Resolve in Task 2: resolve+deny escape with explicit allow-rule escape hatch

open_questions.question: Exact tool arg shapes (read/edit/glob/grep/bash)
open_questions.resolution: Resolve in Task 2: verify against SDK Tool type / existing plugin usage

open_questions.question: Does .opencode/README.md list plugins? (Task 4 doc edit)
open_questions.resolution: Resolve in Task 4: grep README for plugin inventory

stop_conditions: Plugin cannot reliably extract path args from bash (token-scan too brittle) -> drop plugin (t2/t3), keep static invariant (t1)
Plugin false-denies legitimate in-repo work after testing (sc4 fails) -> drop plugin, keep static check, document negative result
opencode upstream fixes bypasses and pins in @opencode-ai/plugin@1.18.4 -> re-evaluate plugin; static invariant stays regardless
non_goals: A permission.ask plugin hook (redundant under deny + locked config)
Permission-event observability/logging (deferred until an audit reason exists)
Rewriting or monkey-patching opencode's own external_directory implementation
Network firewalling, sandboxing beyond path containment, or a separate process jail
Modifying upstream opencode or bumping the pinned SDK
A general bash AST parser (token-scan; accept residual subprocess-in-literal bypass)
